---
description: 'Use when writing commit messages, staging changes, or asked to commit. Covers Conventional Commits format, scopes, multi-commit splitting, and forbidden patterns.'
---

# Commit Conventions

## Format

```
<type>(<scope>): <short description>

<body — required when the subject alone doesn't fully explain the change>
```

- Subject line: imperative mood, lowercase, no period at the end, max 72 chars
- Body: **required** when the commit covers multiple files, a non-obvious fix, or a behaviour change — explain _what_ changed and _why_, not _how_; wrap at 72 chars
- Omit body only when the subject line is fully self-explanatory (e.g. `style: run Prettier`)
- **Never add `Co-authored-by` lines** — ever

## Types

| Type       | When to use                                                |
| ---------- | ---------------------------------------------------------- |
| `feat`     | New user-facing feature or behaviour                       |
| `fix`      | Bug fix                                                    |
| `chore`    | Tooling, deps, config, scripts — no production code change |
| `docs`     | README, ROADMAP, instruction files, comments only          |
| `refactor` | Code restructure with no behaviour change                  |
| `style`    | Formatting only (Prettier, whitespace) — no logic change   |
| `test`     | Adding or fixing tests                                     |
| `perf`     | Performance improvement                                    |

## Scopes

Use one scope per commit. If a commit touches multiple scopes, split it.

| Scope      | Covers                                               |
| ---------- | ---------------------------------------------------- |
| `server`   | Any file under `server/`                             |
| `client`   | Any file under `client/`                             |
| `db`       | Migrations, schema, SQLite setup                     |
| `i18n`     | Locale files, i18n config                            |
| `security` | Rate limiting, CORS, input sanitisation              |
| `config`   | Root `package.json`, `.prettierrc`, `tsconfig`, etc. |
| `ci`       | GitHub Actions, hooks                                |

Scope is **optional** for `chore` and `docs` when the change is repo-wide.

## Multi-commit splitting

When multiple independent concerns are staged, **split into separate commits** — one per scope or concern. Do not bundle unrelated changes.

Examples of correct splits:

```
feat(server): add POST /api/appointments with full validation
feat(client): add BookView form and submission flow
```

```
chore(config): add Prettier with format and format:check scripts
docs: add commits.instructions.md
```

## Examples

```
feat(server): add GET /api/blocked-dates with date range validation
fix(client): prevent past-day selection in DayNavigator on DST boundary
chore(config): configure Tailwind CSS content paths
docs: update ROADMAP statuses after phase 2 completion
refactor(server): extract slot validation into validateSlot helper
style: run Prettier across all ts, vue, and json files
feat(db): add migration 003 to seed Belgian public holidays
fix(security): tighten CORS to use ALLOWED_ORIGINS env var
```

## Forbidden

- No `Co-authored-by: ...` lines
- No `Generated by ...` or `AI-assisted` lines
- No emoji in subject lines
- No vague subjects: ~~`fix stuff`~~, ~~`wip`~~, ~~`update`~~
- No scope that is just a filename: ~~`fix(App.vue)`~~
